title: Dersonn III
content:
{{Top|ca|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image= |name=Dersonn III |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Dersonn system/Legends|Dersonn system]] *[[The Slice/Legends|The Slice]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Dersonn system/Legends|Dersonn system]] |sector=[[Tammuz sector/Legends|Tammuz sector]] |system=[[Dersonn system/Legends|Dersonn system]] |coordinates=T-14 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain= |water= |interest= |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species= |otherspecies= |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports= |affiliation= }} {{Quote|You ever hear of Dersonn III? Or the Iti Cluster colonies?''"
"''Weren't they destroyed?''"
"''Right, that's because Hanharr happened.|Mira and the Jedi Exile|}} '''Dersonn III''' was a terrestrial celestial body located in the [[Dersonn system/Legends|Dersonn system]], a part of the [[Tammuz sector/Legends|Tammuz sector]] in [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. At one point, the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and [[Slaver/Legends|enslaver]] [[Hanharr]], who had previously been [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] himself and harbored a hatred for other living beings, particularly [[Human/Legends|Humans]], caused the destruction of the population of Dersonn III. He also survived a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] crash on the celestial body, a fact that was witnessed by the Human [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Mira]]. {{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords}} In [[3951 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' dates the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' to [[3951 BBY]]. Mira relayed Hanharr's involvement with Dersonn III to the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] known as the [[Meetra Surik|Jedi Exile]], who had previously heard of the destruction of the astronomical object's population. {{Endgame}} ==Behind the scenes== Dersonn III was mentioned in the [[2004]] [[Obsidian Entertainment]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. In the game, the world can be mentioned in an optional conversation the player character, the Jedi Exile, can have with Mira after the former has made a [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side of the Force]]–aligned choice regarding the latter and Hanharr and after Mira has joined their crew as a result. This article assumes one hundred percent game completion—as well as that the player character makes only light side–aligned choices—and that the conversation took place as described. The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Dersonn [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Dersonn III, in grid square T-14. ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{1stm}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |fi=Dersonn III }} [[Category:Astronomical objects in the Outer Rim Territories]] [[Category:Astronomical objects in the Slice]] [[Category:Tammuz sector locations]] [[Category:Terrestrial astronomical objects]]
